annotations.label: Altitude of a triangle

anchor.snippet: an altitude of a triangle is a line segment through a given vertex

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.altitude` defines the altitude of a simplex (through a vertex, orthogonal to the opposite face), which specializes to the triangle altitude.

annotations.label: Base and extended base

anchor.snippet: are called, respectively, the base and extended base of the altitude

annotations.note: Mathlib has no explicit notion named 'base' or 'extended base' of an altitude; the opposite face is referenced via `s.points '' {i}ᶜ` in the altitude definition but is not given this name.

annotations.label: Foot of the altitude

anchor.snippet: is called the foot of the altitude

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.altitudeFoot` is the orthogonal projection of a vertex onto the opposite face, exactly the foot of the altitude.

annotations.label: Length of the altitude (height)

anchor.snippet: is the distance between the foot and the apex

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.height` is defined as `dist (s.points i) (s.altitudeFoot i)`, the length of the altitude.

annotations.label: Dropping the altitude

anchor.snippet: is known as dropping the altitude at that vertex

annotations.note: This is informal terminology for the construction; Mathlib defines the resulting altitude/foot but does not name the action.

annotations.label: Area formula via altitude

anchor.snippet: one-half of the product of an altitude's length and its base's length

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of triangle area as ½·base·height; no such formula was found.

annotations.label: Longest altitude perpendicular to shortest side

anchor.snippet: the longest altitude is perpendicular to the shortest side of the triangle

annotations.note: No lemma comparing the lengths of distinct altitudes to the lengths of the opposite sides was found.

annotations.label: Isosceles altitude properties

anchor.snippet: the altitude having the incongruent side as its base will have the midpoint of that side as its foot

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `Isosceles` triangle predicate or lemma identifying the altitude foot with the midpoint of the base.

annotations.label: Isosceles altitude is angle bisector

anchor.snippet: the altitude having the incongruent side as its base will be the angle bisector of the vertex angle

annotations.note: No isosceles-triangle altitude/angle-bisector coincidence lemma found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric mean theorem

anchor.snippet: the altitude drawn to the hypotenuse c divides the hypotenuse into two segments

annotations.note: Searches for 'geometric mean theorem' and related right-triangle altitude identities returned no matches in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Location of altitude feet by triangle type

anchor.snippet: For acute triangles, the feet of the altitudes all fall on the triangle's sides

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Affine.Simplex.AcuteAngled` but has no theorem characterizing where altitude feet lie relative to the sides by acute/right/obtuse classification.

annotations.label: Orthocenter

anchor.snippet: is the point where the three (possibly extended) altitudes intersect

annotations.note: `Affine.Triangle.orthocenter` is defined via the Monge point and shown to lie on all altitudes (`orthocenter_mem_altitude`).

annotations.label: Orthocenter location characterization

anchor.snippet: The orthocenter lies inside the triangle if and only if the triangle is acute

annotations.note: No lemma relating membership of the orthocenter in the triangle's interior to acuteness/obtuseness is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthocenter of right triangle

anchor.snippet: the orthocenter coincides with the vertex at the right angle

annotations.note: No statement identifying the orthocenter of a right triangle with the right-angle vertex was found.

annotations.label: Orthocenter of equilateral triangle

anchor.snippet: all triangle centers (including the orthocenter) coincide at its centroid

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Equilateral` and `centroid`/`orthocenter`/`circumcenter`/`incenter` but no lemma stating they coincide for an equilateral triangle.

annotations.label: Altitude in terms of the sides

anchor.section: Altitude in terms of the sides
anchor.snippet: the altitude from side a (the base) is given by

annotations.note: No formula expressing the altitude length in terms of side lengths (Heron-style) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Altitudes inversely proportional to sides

anchor.section: Altitude in terms of the sides
anchor.snippet: Any two altitudes of a triangle are inversely proportional with the sides on which they fall

annotations.note: No lemma stating `hₐ · a = h_b · b` (twice area) for triangle altitudes was found.

annotations.label: Inradius theorem

anchor.snippet: The altitudes and the incircle radius r are related by

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Affine.Simplex.inradius` (in `Geometry/Euclidean/Incenter.lean`) but has no lemma relating it to the sum of reciprocals of the altitudes.

annotations.label: Circumradius theorem

anchor.snippet: and the triangle's circumradius (radius of the triangle's circumscribed circle) as R , the altitude is given by

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Affine.Simplex.circumradius` but no formula expressing an altitude in terms of two sides and the circumradius.

annotations.label: Interior point altitude relation

anchor.snippet: are the perpendicular distances from any point P to the sides

annotations.note: No theorem of the form `y₁/hₐ + y₂/h_b + y₃/h_c = 1` for distances from an interior point was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area reciprocal theorem

anchor.snippet: the semi-sum of the reciprocals of the altitudes

annotations.note: No area formula involving the reciprocals of the altitudes was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General point on an altitude

anchor.snippet: If E is any point on an altitude AD of any triangle

annotations.note: No identity `AC² + EB² = AB² + CE²` for points on an altitude was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triangle inequality for altitudes

anchor.snippet: the triangle inequality

annotations.note: No lemma asserting that the altitudes of a triangle themselves satisfy the triangle inequality was found.

annotations.label: Viviani's theorem

anchor.snippet: the sum of the perpendiculars to the three sides is equal to the altitude of the triangle

annotations.note: A grep for 'Viviani' returned no results in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Legs as altitudes in right triangle

anchor.snippet: each of the legs is also an altitude

annotations.note: No statement identifying the legs of a right triangle with two of its altitudes was found.

annotations.label: Inverse Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: This is also known as the inverse Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: A grep for 'inverse Pythagorean' returned no results; only the standard Pythagorean theorem is present in Mathlib.
